name: NP-Completeness via Reduction
description: To prove a problem NP-complete one shows it lies in NP and is NP-hard: membership is shown with a polynomial-time verifier, then a known NP-complete problem is reduced to it by a polynomial-time mapping that preserves yes-instances; verifying the reduction's size and correctness completes the proof.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Target problem X"]
    N2["Show X in NP"]
    N3{"In NP?"}
    N4["Not NP-complete here"]
    N5["Pick known NPC Y"]
    N6["Construct reduction"]
    N7["Map Y instance to X"]
    N8{"Reduction polynomial?"}
    N9["Fix mapping"]
    N10{"Y yes iff X yes?"}
    N11["Repair correctness"]
    N12["X is NP-complete"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|no| N4
    N4 -->|result| N12
    N3 -->|yes| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|retry| N6
    N8 -->|yes| N10
    N10 -->|no| N11
    N11 -->|rework| N6
    N10 -->|yes| N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: The complexity of theorem-proving procedures
sources.journal: ACM Symposium on Theory of Computing (STOC)
